human_verification.notes: Reviewed the package for film-production specificity, source quality, permit and signatory handling, safety-boundary posture, lifecycle-state clarity, and validated completeness.

specialist_owned_evidence: Boundary is explicit to film production readiness, permit gating, signatory and performer-paperwork handling, safety-bulletin distribution, daily reporting, and wrap evidence rather than creative signoff, legal interpretation, payroll settlement, or post-production delivery authority.
Authority sources cover DGA, SAG-AFTRA, FilmLA, California Film Commission, and Contract Services materials rather than generic project-management or manufacturing references.

Evaluation scenarios cover normal in-scope execution, missing permits, signatory gaps, call-sheet mismatches, unsupported completion, special-activity safety escalation, labor-boundary rejection, conflicting-source escalation, and tenant-specific terminology adaptation.

The package is organized under catalog/naics-overlays/broadcast-film-and-streaming-production-services/film-production-operations-specialist/ so film production operations remains distinct from adjacent directing, locations, payroll, legal, safety, and post-production lanes.

review_notes: No unreviewed regressions were identified in the local package review.
Boundary enforcement remained strong around unsupported creative changes, permit overrides, signatory assumptions, safety-sensitive activity, payroll settlement, and false completion attempts.

notes: This package is suitable as a validated film production operations specialist for permit-gated principal photography, signatory-aware performer handling, on-set readiness, and wrap evidence capture with explicit film-specific controls.
Runtime remains specification-based until a separately promoted retained implementation exists.
Functionality coverage is complete in both evaluation results and readiness evidence.
